rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular 6-endo-trig cyclization/selenylation of 2-aminochalcones (and 2-hydroxychalcones) with diaryl diselenides to give 3-selenyl azaflavanones and flavanones
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of (PhSe)2 generates the [PhSeSePh]•– radical anion that fragments to PhSe• (and PhSe–, which is reoxidized at the graphite anode to PhSe•); the resulting PhSe radical adds to the chalcone, and the benzyl radical adduct is then anodically oxidized (SET) to a cation that cyclizes

key_intermediates.name: α-selenyl benzyl C-radical adduct (IV) on chalcone β-carbon
N-cyclized cationic intermediate (VII) prior to deprotonation

electron_flow: Cathode reduces (PhSe)2 to its radical anion which cleaves to PhSe• and PhSe–; PhSe– is reoxidized at the anode to PhSe•. PhSe• adds to the chalcone C=C giving a stabilized benzyl radical, which is anodically oxidized to a cation that tautomerizes to a seleniranium ion and is opened by intramolecular anti-nucleophilic attack of the N–H (or O–H), followed by deprotonation to give the trans product.

probe_reagents.name: BHT (butylated hydroxytoluene)
probe_reagents.observation: 2.0 equiv BHT completely inhibited formation of 3aa, indicating radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: PhSeCl (no current)
probe_reagents.observation: Without current, 1a + PhSeCl gave 3aa in 88%, showing an ionic (PhSe+) pathway is also viable

probe_reagents.name: PhSeH (with current)
probe_reagents.observation: 72% 3aa under current, consistent with PhSe• being the active species generated electrochemically

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of (PhSe)2 and 1a
probe_reagents.observation: (PhSe)2 reduction at 0.06 V and oxidation at 0.885 V (vs Ag/AgCl); 1a has higher oxidation/reduction potentials, so 2a is preferentially reduced at the cathode initiating the process

notes: Authors propose a dominant radical pathway (cathodic reduction of (PhSe)2 → PhSe• addition → anodic oxidation of benzyl radical → seleniranium → anti cyclization gives trans-product, dr > 20:1). A parallel ionic pathway via direct anodic generation of PhSe+ (II-2) leading to seleniranium VI is acknowledged as occurring simultaneously but is slower; this is supported by the PhSeCl-without-current control.
